steps.description: Audit the existing unified document route implementations and shared helpers, then refactor the shared utilities and visibility middleware in a narrowly scoped way so the new CRUD behavior can reuse current code instead of duplicating logic. Keep the work constrained to the existing API route stack to avoid the large typecheck regression from the prior attempt.

Implement the unified documents CRUD surface in the existing documents router: list by workspace with type/parent/visibility filtering, create with Zod validation for all requested entity types and belongs_to associations, load single documents with Yjs fallback, patch with tracked-field history + issue state timestamp updates, and soft-delete via deleted_at. Reuse the extracted utilities and avoid introducing unrelated route shape changes.

Tighten the dedicated issues router to match the requested unified-document behavior without broad rewrites: list issues with assignee join and batched belongs_to lookup, create issues with per-workspace ticket_number allocation and requested enums, and patch issues with history logging plus started/completed/cancelled/reopened timestamp updates.

Bring the dedicated programs, projects, and weeks routers into the same unified-document CRUD pattern, reusing the shared helpers and preserving their existing response shapes where possible to minimize fallout. Focus only on the endpoints requested by the user and the issue-history/timestamp behavior that overlaps with the shared document logic.

Update the associations and search routers to support the requested document_associations management and mention/learning search semantics, including visibility-aware lookups and escaped LIKE matching. Keep changes localized to these two routers plus any shared helper reuse.

Adjust app wiring and API schema registration only if needed after the route changes, keeping the scope minimal. Update route mounting and OpenAPI schema definitions for documents/issues/programs/projects/search/weeks only where the new request/response shapes require it.

Update the focused API tests that cover the requested verification path—document create/get/patch/history/search/delete plus the touched issue/program/project/week/association/search behaviors—rather than attempting broad test-suite churn. Limit test edits to the route specs directly exercising the changed endpoints to reduce the risk of another massive typecheck explosion.

messages.content:
Implement the unified document CRUD API routes for Ship. All entity types (wiki, issue, program, project, sprint, person, weekly_plan, weekly_retro) are rows in a single `documents` table differentiated by `document_type`.

Create `routes/documents.ts` with auth middleware on all routes: GET / (list documents by workspace, filter by type/parent, respect visibility: workspace docs visible to all, private docs visible to creator and admins only), POST / (create document with Zod validation for title, document_type, parent_id, properties, visibility, content as TipTap JSON, belongs_to associations array), GET /:id (single document with visibility check, load content from yjs_state via Yjs-to-JSON converter if available, fallback to content column), PATCH /:id (partial update with document_history logging for tracked fields like title/state/priority/assignee_id, timestamp updates for issue state transitions: started_at/completed_at/cancelled_at/reopened_at), DELETE /:id (soft delete via deleted_at).

Create `routes/issues.ts` with: GET / (list issues with assignee name join, belongs_to associations batch lookup), POST / (create issue with auto-incrementing ticket_number per workspace, state enum: triage/backlog/todo/in_progress/in_review/done/cancelled, priority enum: urgent/high/medium/low/none), PATCH /:id (update with state transition timestamps and history logging).

Create `routes/programs.ts`, `routes/projects.ts`, and `routes/weeks.ts` following the same pattern. Create `routes/associations.ts` for managing document_associations (link/unlink documents to programs/projects/sprints). Create `routes/search.ts` with mention search (people + documents, trigram LIKE with SQL wildcard escape) and learning search.

Extract shared utilities to `utils/document-crud.ts`: logDocumentChange (inserts to document_history), getTimestampUpdates (maps state changes to timestamp columns), getBelongsToAssociations (fetches associations for a document). Create `middleware/visibility.ts` with VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL helper and isWorkspaceAdmin check.

Verify: create a document via POST, retrieve it via GET, update title via PATCH, confirm history entry in document_history, search for it via /api/search/mentions, soft-delete via DELETE, confirm GET returns 404.

[Plan Warning] This plan has 16 steps targeting 18 files. Consider decomposing into smaller runs.

Audited the requested route files and shared helpers as source of truth. Here are the precise gaps I found against the requested unified CRUD/search behavior, without editing yet.

## High-confidence gaps to close

### 1) `api/src/utils/document-crud.ts` is not yet the full shared foundation
Current helpers exist, but they don’t fully cover the requested unified behavior:

- `TRACKED_FIELDS` is present, but only includes:
  - `title`, `state`, `priority`, `assignee_id`, `estimate`, `belongs_to`
- Missing from shared coverage for unified document flows:
  - document-level field history for non-issue updates where needed
  - reusable helpers for computing issue state transition timestamps in route flow
  - reusable helpers for loading belongs_to associations in the exact shapes needed for:
    - single document fetch
    - batch document fetch
    - route-specific flattening/shape normalization
- `getTimestampUpdates()` exists, but the route files are not yet consistently using it for issue state transitions.
- `getBelongsToAssociations()` / `getBelongsToAssociationsBatch()` exist, but need confirmation against route expectations:
  - current single-fetch route implementations often hand-roll association loading
  - current shapes differ by route (`belongs_to` objects vs program/project embedding vs context breadcrumb use)

### 2) `api/src/middleware/visibility.ts` is conceptually correct, but route usage is inconsistent
The middleware exposes:
- `isWorkspaceAdmin()`
- `canAccessVisibleDocument()`
- `getVisibilityContext()`
- `VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL()`

The core rule is already encoded correctly:
- workspace docs readable by all workspace members
- private docs readable by creator or admin

But the gap is implementation consistency:
- some routes use `VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL(...)`
- some route logic still duplicates access checks manually
- admin reusability is not yet consistently centralized in route handlers
- private-vs-workspace behavior may still diverge in individual route SQL conditions

### 3) `api/src/routes/documents.ts` is the biggest mismatch for unified document CRUD
This route already has a lot of logic, but it does **not yet satisfy the requested unified `/api/documents` behavior** cleanly.

#### Observed gaps
- Create/list/get/update/delete behavior is mixed with legacy/conversion/collaboration concerns.
- Auth is present on main endpoints, but behavior is not yet clearly unified across all document CRUD paths.
- List behavior:
  - filters by workspace, type, parent_id, archived/deleted, visibility
  - but does not yet obviously support the full requested CRUD/search contract in a single coherent API shape
- Create behavior:
  - has validation for many fields
  - but the requested step explicitly calls out:
    - TipTap JSON
    - belongs_to
    - all requested fields
  - need to confirm whether create/update payloads cover the exact schema expected by downstream APIs
- Single-fetch behavior:
  - visibility check exists
  - Yjs fallback is mentioned/imported
  - but the route needs exact unified fallback semantics for missing content
- Patch behavior:
  - currently includes broad field handling
  - but issue-field tracking/timestamps/history handling is not yet clearly delegated to shared CRUD helpers
- Delete behavior:
  - current route behavior is not the requested “soft-delete via `deleted_at`”
  - the step explicitly requires: soft-delete so subsequent GET returns 404
  - current file appears to contain legacy/archival/conversion behavior that may not match that expectation

#### Specific likely mismatch areas
- `canAccessDocument()` is local and may not align perfectly with shared visibility helper usage.
- `belongs_to` association handling is partly in-route rather than unified through shared helpers.
- issue field changes and timestamps likely need stricter shared handling.
- document history logging is imported elsewhere in the codebase but not clearly integrated here in the required way.
- legacy redirect/conversion behavior in GET by id is likely outside the requested unified CRUD contract unless explicitly preserved.

### 4) `api/src/routes/issues.ts` already uses shared CRUD helpers, but behavior still needs reconciliation
This route is closer to the desired shared pattern, but gaps remain relative to the requested next-step behavior:

- It imports and uses:
  - `logDocumentChange`
  - `getTimestampUpdates`
  - `getBelongsToAssociations`
  - `getBelongsToAssociationsBatch`
  - `TRACKED_FIELDS`
- That’s good, but the route still needs confirmation that:
  - every tracked issue mutation logs history consistently
  - state transitions compute timestamps exactly as required
  - associations are loaded in the exact shapes expected by issue APIs
- The route’s existing logic suggests it may be partly aligned already, but it is not yet the final canonical implementation the next step describes.

### 5) `api/src/routes/programs.ts` and `api/src/routes/projects.ts` are still bespoke document-backed APIs
Both routes already operate on unified `documents` rows, but they still have custom, route-specific implementations.

#### Programs route gaps
- Uses visibility helper correctly.
- Still performs its own CRUD over `documents` rather than leaning on a shared document CRUD surface.
- Delete currently hard-deletes from `documents` and `document_associations`, which may not align with unified soft-delete expectations if the route is supposed to preserve document records.

#### Projects route gaps
- Also uses visibility helper correctly.
- Has extensive custom business logic around project retro, ICE scoring, plan generation, and completeness.
- Needs reconciliation with unified document CRUD semantics if these routes should conform to a shared document model.
- The route imports shared types/helpers from `@ship/shared`, but the CRUD pathway is still bespoke.

### 6) `api/src/routes/weeks.ts` is heavily custom and likely only partially aligned
This file contains a lot of week/sprint-specific logic:
- lookup endpoints
- sprint owner resolution
- plan/approval/review logic
- snapshotting
- retro flows

That’s all document-backed, but it is not yet obviously normalized to the requested unified CRUD + shared association/visibility behavior.

Likely gaps:
- association loading shape/visibility consistency
- document history/timestamp consistency for issue-backed operations in week flows
- reuse of shared helpers instead of bespoke query logic where this step expects it

### 7) `api/src/routes/associations.ts` has correct visibility gating, but limited abstraction
This route already:
- enforces auth
- checks document access
- uses `VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL`
- supports list/create/delete/reverse-context patterns

Gaps relative to the next steps:
- it may still rely on bespoke access checks instead of a single shared visibility rule helper everywhere
- no broader unified CRUD behavior is implemented here; it’s still a dedicated association route

### 8) `api/src/routes/search.ts` uses visibility well, but it’s not yet a unified document search surface
Current search behavior:
- mentions search
- learnings search
- workspace visibility filtering

Gaps:
- not a generalized unified document search endpoint
- search result shapes are route-specific
- may need to align with the document visibility rule and any unified document listing/search expectations if the next steps require that

## Summary of the exact closure areas

If I condense this to the smallest actionable gap list, it is:

1. **Move shared issue/document CRUD concerns into `document-crud.ts` more completely**
   - history logging usage
   - timestamp transition handling
   - belongs_to fetch helpers in the exact route-needed shapes

2. **Standardize visibility enforcement**
   - use the shared visibility rule consistently in all document-backed routes
   - keep admin check reusable from route handlers

3. **Reconcile `documents.ts` with requested unified CRUD semantics**
   - auth on all endpoints
   - create/list/get/patch/delete to match the desired unified shape
   - soft delete via `deleted_at`
   - Yjs fallback on fetch
   - partial update + history + timestamps for tracked issue fields
   - exact `belongs_to` handling
   - avoid legacy behavior where it conflicts with unified CRUD

4. **Ensure downstream routes consume the shared helpers consistently**
   - issues
   - programs
   - projects
   - weeks
   - associations
   - search

No files edited in this step, per instruction to audit before modifying.

[Plan Warning] This plan has 8 steps targeting 22 files. Consider decomposing into smaller runs.

Implemented the shared helper refactor for the current step:

- `visibility.ts`
  - Expanded `canAccessVisibleDocument` to explicitly handle:
    - workspace-visible documents
    - private documents owned by creator
    - admin overrides
    - safer fallback behavior for unknown visibility values

- `document-crud.ts`
  - Added `BelongsToSyncOptions`
  - Enhanced `syncBelongsToAssociations` to:

Instruction: Implement the unified document CRUD API routes for Ship. All entity types (wiki, issue, program, project, sprint, person, weekly_plan, weekly_retro) are rows in a single `documents` table differentiated by `document_type`.

Create `routes/documents.ts` with auth middleware on all routes: GET / (list documents by workspace, filter by type/parent, respect visibility: workspace docs visible to all, private docs visible to creator and admins only), POST / (create document with Zod validation for title, document_type, parent_id, properties, visibility, content as TipTap JSON, belongs_to associations array), GET /:id (single document with visibility check, load content from yjs_state via Yjs-to-JSON converter if available, fallback to content column), PATCH /:id (partial update with document_history logging for tracked fields like title/state/priority/assignee_id, timestamp updates for issue state transitions: started_at/completed_at/cancelled_at/reopened_at), DELETE /:id (soft delete via deleted_at).

Create `routes/issues.ts` with: GET / (list issues with assignee name join, belongs_to associations batch lookup), POST / (create issue with auto-incrementing ticket_number per workspace, state enum: triage/backlog/todo/in_progress/in_review/done/cancelled, priority enum: urgent/high/medium/low/none), PATCH /:id (update with state transition timestamps and history logging).

Create `routes/programs.ts`, `routes/projects.ts`, and `routes/weeks.ts` following the same pattern. Create `routes/associations.ts` for managing document_associations (link/unlink documents to programs/projects/sprints). Create `routes/search.ts` with mention search (people + documents, trigram LIKE with SQL wildcard escape) and learning search.

Extract shared utilities to `utils/document-crud.ts`: logDocumentChange (inserts to document_history), getTimestampUpdates (maps state changes to timestamp columns), getBelongsToAssociations (fetches associations for a document). Create `middleware/visibility.ts` with VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL helper and isWorkspaceAdmin check.

Verify: create a document via POST, retrieve it via GET, update title via PATCH, confirm history entry in document_history, search for it via /api/search/mentions, soft-delete via DELETE, confirm GET returns 404.
